segments.text: Lesson 53.

translation.text: 第53课。

segments.text: In the Public Interest.

translation.text: 为了公众利益。

segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translation.text: 听录音，然后回答以下问题。

segments.text: What could not be reported in the official files?

translation.text: 什么情况无法记录在官方档案中？

segments.text: The Scandinavian countries are much admired all over the world for their enlightened social policies.

translation.text: 斯堪的纳维亚各国因其开明的社会政策而受到全世界赞赏。

segments.text: Sweden has evolved an excellent system for protecting the individual citizen from high-handed or incompetent public officers.

translation.text: 瑞典逐渐建立了一套出色制度，保护公民个人免受专横或无能的公务人员侵害。

segments.text: The system has worked so well that it has been adopted in other countries, too.

translation.text: 这套制度成效卓著，其他国家也采用了它。

segments.text: The Swedes were the first to recognize that public officials like civil servants, police officers, health inspectors, or tax collectors can make mistakes or act overzealously in the belief that they are

translation.text: 瑞典人最先认识到，公务员、警察、卫生检查员或税务人员等公职人员可能犯错，也可能因为自认为在

segments.text: serving the public.

translation.text: 为公众服务而行动过度。

segments.text: As long ago as 1809, the Swedish parliament introduced a scheme to safeguard the interest of the individual.

translation.text: 早在1809年，瑞典议会就推出了一项保护个人利益的制度。

segments.text: A parliamentary committee representing all political parties appoints person who is suitably qualified to investigate private grievances against the state.

translation.text: 一个代表所有政党的议会委员会任命一位资质适当的人，调查私人对国家机构的申诉。

segments.text: official title of the person is Justite Ombudsman, but the Swedes commonly refer to him as the J.O.

translation.text: 此人的正式职称是司法监察专员，但瑞典人通常称他为J.O.，

segments.text: or Ombudsman.

translation.text: 或直接称监察专员。

segments.text: The Ombudsman is not subject to political pressure.

translation.text: 监察专员不受政治压力支配。

segments.text: He investigates complaints, large and small, that come to him from all levels of society.

translation.text: 他调查来自社会各阶层的大大小小的投诉。

segments.text: As complaints must be made in writing, the Ombudsman receives an average of 1,200 letters a year.

translation.text: 由于投诉必须书面提出，监察专员平均每年收到1200封信。

segments.text: He has eight lawyer assistants to help him, and he examines every single letter in detail.

translation.text: 他有八名律师助理协助，并逐封仔细审查每一封信。

segments.text: There is nothing secretive about the Ombudsman's work, for his correspondence is open to public inspection.

translation.text: 监察专员的工作毫不神秘，因为他的往来函件可供公众查阅。

segments.text: If a citizen's complaint is justified, the Ombudsman will act on his behalf.

translation.text: 如果公民的投诉理由充分，监察专员便会代表他采取行动。

segments.text: The action he takes varies according to the nature of the complaint.

translation.text: 他采取何种行动，视投诉性质而定。

segments.text: He may gently reprimand an official, or even suggest to parliament that a law be altered.

translation.text: 他可能温和地训诫一名官员，甚至建议议会修改法律。

segments.text: The following case is a typical example of the Ombudsman's work.

translation.text: 下面的案件是监察专员工作的一个典型例子。

segments.text: A foreigner living in a Swedish village wrote to the Ombudsman complaining that he had been ill-treated by the police simply because he was a foreigner.

translation.text: 一名住在瑞典某村庄的外国人写信给监察专员，投诉警察仅因他是外国人便粗暴对待他。

segments.text: The Ombudsman immediately wrote to the chief of police in the district, asking him to send a record of the case.

translation.text: 监察专员立即致信该地区警察局长，请他送交这起案件的记录。

segments.text: There was nothing in the record to show that the foreigner's complaint was justified, and the chief of police strongly denied the accusation.

translation.text: 记录中没有任何证据表明这名外国人的投诉有理，警察局长也坚决否认指控。

segments.text: It was impossible for the Ombudsman to take action, but when he received a similar complaint from another foreigner in the same village, he immediately sent one of his lawyers to investigate the matter.

translation.text: 监察专员无法采取行动，但收到同村另一名外国人的类似投诉后，他立即派一名律师调查此事。

segments.text: The lawyer ascertained that a policeman had indeed dealt roughly with foreigners on several occasions.

translation.text: 律师查明，一名警察确实曾多次粗暴对待外国人。

segments.text: The fact that the policeman was prejudiced against foreigners could not be recorded in the official files.

translation.text: 这名警察对外国人抱有偏见的事实无法记入官方档案。

segments.text: It was only possible for the Ombudsman to find this out by sending one of his representatives to check the facts.

translation.text: 监察专员只有派代表核查事实，才可能查明这一点。

segments.text: The policeman in question was severely reprimanded and was informed that if any further complaints were lodged against him, he would be prosecuted.

translation.text: 涉事警察受到严厉训诫，并被告知如果再有人投诉，他将受到起诉。

segments.text: The Ombudsman's prompt action at once put an end to an unpleasant practice which might have gone unnoticed.

translation.text: 监察专员迅速采取行动，立即制止了这种本可能一直无人察觉的恶劣做法。
